title: S'kytri

The '''S'kytri''' (meaning '''the Windborn''' in [[S'kytric|their language]]) were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of winged [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] indigenous to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Skye/Legends|Skye]], also known to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] as Marat V. They were normally tall, muscular and thin, which is why they were regarded as one of the most beautiful species in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Throughout history, the S'kytri had built up a reputation of noble warrior bards.

==Biology==
[[File:9council.jpg|thumb|left|180px|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] meet a group of S'kytri]]
The S'kytri from [[Skye/Legends|Skye]] were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of tall, [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Mammal/Legends|mammals]]. In many respects, they looked similar to [[Human/Legends|Humans]],<ref name="uaa144" /> the most common species in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]<ref name="NEGAS">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref>: they had [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] bodies with two [[Limb/Legends|arms]] ending with five-fingered hands, two legs with five-toed feet, and a face that featured a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]], a [[Nose/Legends|nose]] and two forward-facing [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. The rest of their features, however, set them clearly apart from their distant Human cousins. The most striking differences were their large, apparently membranous wings, their decidedly non-Human skintones and their towering height.<ref name="uaa144" /> While the average Human only grew to 1.70 [[Meter/Legends|meters]], many S'kytri surpassed two meters, and those under 1.80 meters were considered short.<ref name="GM1" />

As they were a flying species, the S'kytri regarded excessive body weight as both impractical and dangerous, which is why they were all thin and brawny. Additionally, their [[Skeleton/Legends|skeletons]] were hollow, much like a [[Bird/Legends|bird's]], which allowed them easier flight. Because of that and despite their exotic features, they were deemed beautiful by many other species in the galaxy.<ref name="GM1" />

While most mammals [[Reproduction/Legends|gave birth]] to live [[Youngling/Legends|young]], the S'kytri hatched from [[Egg/Legends|eggs]]. [[Gender/Legends|Female]] S'kytri were born with green skin while males were born with blue. Any egg that was perceived to have a female with blue skin or a male with green was perceived as an abomination, and was thus immediately [[Death/Legends|killed]]. Their [[Hair/Legends|hair]] [[Color/Legends|color]] ranged from moonlight blond to jet black, and their eyes from dark blue to light silver and every other color in between.<ref name="uaa144" />

They started adolescence at twelve years of age and were considered biologically mature at seventeen. Beginning at around forty years, a S'kytri entered middle age, with seniority being reached at sixty-five. A S'kytri's life expectancy was about seventy-five standard years, while those above that age were held to be venerable.<ref name="uaa144" />

==Society and culture==
The S'kytri were grouped into clans, which were themselves divided in three groups, the [[Lowland Clans|Lowland]] and [[Highland Clans]], as well as the banished [[Outland Clan]].<ref name="swcw">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref> The members of each group bore distinctive markings. Those clans dwelled in cities built in the [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] and were governed by a [[Supreme Council of the Highland Clans|Supreme Council]].<ref name="TLH">[[Star Wars Annual (1977) 1|''Star Wars Annual'' (1977) 1]]</ref> The position of [[Patriarch (Skye)|Patriarch]], leader of all the S'kytri, was awarded alternately to the leader of either Councils. Once given the position, a clan's leader would hold it for a 22-year-long term, after which they were required to hand the title to the other clan.<ref name="GM1" /> In the normal course of events, the Windborn disapproved of violence, but they proved to be able and courageous warriors when necessary. A strong tendency to be arrogant was present in their mentality. Because of the many possibilities their wings offered, the S'kytri regarded species that were incapable of natural flight as inferior. That cultural trait was strongly marked in the behavior of the young adults, who also tended to be disobedient and bold.<ref name="uaa144" />

==History==
The S'kytri evolved on [[Skye/Legends|Skye]],<ref name="uaa144" /> a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the [[Varada sector/Legends|Varada sector]], in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] region of the galaxy.<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Their first contact with the wider [[galactic community]] dated back to as early as the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]], a conflict that lasted from [[7003 BBY|7003]] to approximately [[6900 BBY]]. By that [[Time/Legends|time]], some S'kytri had become [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] under the command of [[Remulus Dreypa]], a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] who had fallen to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and had been exiled by [[Jedi Order/Legends|his order]].<ref name="LOTSS2">[[Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral 2|''Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral'' 2]]</ref>

At the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], a pan-galactic conflict between the Galactic Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Skye was put under threat by a genetic terrorist, [[Zeta Magnus]], who attempted to devastate the planet. The Jedi decided to intervene. Three [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] warriors, [[Halagad Ventor]], [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], [[Battle of Skye|rescued]] the planet from impending doom.<ref name="Aliens in the Empire">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/96/indexp2.html|series=Aliens in the Empire|text=Part 1: Hierarchy of Power}}</ref> Following those events, the S'kytri were unswervingly loyal to the Jedi.<ref name="uaa144" />

At the conclusion of the war in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] of the Republic [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] revealed his true colors, turning the democratic government into an autocratic [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and ordering the [[Order 66/Legends|extermination]] of all Jedi.<ref name="ROS">{{Film|III}}</ref> Because of the newly-formed Empire's nominal [[speciesism]]<ref name="Aliens in the Empire" /> and because of the strong relationship they had maintained with the Jedi, the S'kytri were condemned to [[Slavery/Legends|servitude]]. The female named [[Kharys]], who had witnessed the Jedi intervention at the age of thirteen, allied herself with the Empire and placed in charge of her people by Darth Vader, the [[Armor/Legends|armored]] right-hand man of the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] Palpatine. Vader was none other than Anakin Skywalker, who had turned to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|forces of evil]], and he trained Kharys in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], including [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]]. Under the rule of that new Majestrix Kharys, who enforced the Imperial doctrine, the Windborn endured long years of slavery.<ref name="uaa144" />

==S'kytri in the galaxy==
Following the end of the Hundred-Year Darkness, a contingent of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] that included several S'kytri crashed on the world of [[Kesh/Legends|Kesh]]. Around [[2974 BBY]], the all-Forceful descendants of the marooned Jedi—collectively known as [[the Doomed]]—were led by [[Kaliska (S'kytri)|Kaliska]], the last known remaining member of the S'kytri species within their community.<ref name="LOTSS2" />

==Behind the scenes==
Though continuity specifies that only blue or green-skinned S'kytri are ever born, the ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'' features an image of a red-skinned female. This discrepancy was later retconned by the creation of the red-skinned Outland Clan in the novella ''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]].''

In issues [[Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral 3|#3]], [[Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral 4|#4]] and [[Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral 5|#5]] of [[Star Wars: Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral|''Star Wars: Lost Tribe of the Sith—Spiral'']], S'kytri is misspelled as Sky'tri.
